# New Epics to Implement

## Secondary sort has no impact on the order of results

On the Universe Screen when I click "Avg Purch Yield%" and then Shift + Click "Yield%" such that both are descending, the order of results is the same as when I just click "Avg Purch Yield%" with the order of items in "Yield%" once there is no longer an "Avg Purch Yield%" to sort by being random, or more likely the order the records exist in the database.  This is not the expected behavior, as the secondary sort should be applied when there are ties in the primary sort, but it seems that the secondary sort is not being applied at all.

I can see that the client is sending the correct information to the server, but it seems like the server is not applying the secondary sort at all, so we need to investigate and fix this issue on the server side to ensure that the secondary sort is applied correctly when there are ties in the primary sort.

Make sure you use the Playwright MCP server to verify your fix and to create tests that verify the secondary sort continues to work correctly in the future.

## Janky scrolling issue still exists

You were supposed to have fixes the Janky scrolling issue in Epic 31, you can refer to it for more details. The issue continues across all tables in the app.

Make sure you use the Playwright MCP server to reproduce the issue first and then to verify that you've fixed the issue once you've implemented the fix. Continue fixing and checking until the issue is resolved.

## Dividend Retrieval

We updated the dividend retrieval code to use dividendhistory.org instead of Yahoo Finance, but I still see dividend code for Yahoo Finance.

There should not be any code in Yahoo Finance that retrieves dividend information, including current dividend. ALL dividend information should now be retrieved from dividendhistory.org, so you need to remove any code that retrieves dividend information from Yahoo Finance and make sure that all dividend information is being retrieved from dividendhistory.org. This includes current dividend information that is displayed on the universe screen and the account screens, as well as any dividend information that is used for calculations or other purposes in the app. Make sure to test your changes thoroughly to ensure that all dividend information is being retrieved correctly from dividendhistory.org and that there are no issues with the new implementation.

## Column Filters On Universe Screen

Looking at the column filters on the Universe screen, the inputs are clipped by the width of the column rather than being constrained by the width of the column. This is handled correctly on the Account > Open Positions screen and the Account > Sold Positions screen, so you should probably look there for a fix.

Be sure to use the Playwright MCP server to verify your fix, and make sure to test the column filters on the Universe screen thoroughly to ensure that they are no longer clipped by the width of the column and that they are functioning correctly.

## Lazy Loading of Data

In Epic 40 you were supposed to have implemented lazy loading of data on all the tables and create e2e tests to verify that it works. But it still loads the first 10 and then loads ALL the remaining rows regardless of how many rows are in the viewport. This is a HUGE problem because we could end up having YEARS of data, especially in the Dividend Deposits screen. Loading ALL the data all at once would create an untenable performance issue, not to mention being a memory hog.

Make sure you use the Playwright MCP server to verify your fix and look at the Network traffic in the browser dev tools to ensure that we are only loading data as it scrolls into the viewport and that we are not loading all the remaining rows at once after the first 10. Continue fixing and checking until the lazy loading of data is working correctly on all tables.

## CSV Import and Splits

The CSV import functionality is almost correct but it is not dealing with splits at all.

For example, OXLC had a reverse split on 2025-09-08. The entry in the CSV file has the description as "REVERSE SPLIT R/S FROM 691543102#REOR M005168075001".  The symbol is OXLC and the Quantity is 306.  Out the time we had 1530 shares we had purchased but not sold yet. From this we know it was a 1 for 5 reverse split, so we should have ended up with 306 shares after the split, but instead, we have 1530 shares after the split, which is incorrect. We need to fix the CSV import functionality to correctly handle splits based on the information provided in the description of the CSV entry.

The way I would like to deal with splits is by adjust the price and quantity of the existing records and then dealing with any remainders as a sale.

Other things I know from the description is the number 691543102 is the CUSIP number for the security. Since we resolve the CUSIP to a ticker symbol and we have that already in this row, we probably don't need that information. It may be enough that the description contains the word "SPLIT" in it to know that it is a split and we can use the quantity before and after to figure the ratio to change the price and quantity of the current records.

There is often an additional row that deals with the remainder of shares of the split would cause fractional shares, this goes into the cash balance. For OXLC, there is a line, "IN LIEU OF FRX SHARE EU PAYOUT..." with an amount that we should treat as a sell. But since we are already dealing with remainders as a sale, we may not need to do anything other than intentionally ignoring this line.
